sections.paragraph:
f., with suff. אֶצְבָּעִי; pl. אֶצְבָּעֹות (for צְבַע, with Aleph prosthetic).

(1) a finger, Exo 31:18 specially the fore-finger, which is used in dipping into anything (from the root צָבַע); Lev 4:6 seq.; 14:16 Exo 8:15, אֶצְבַּע אֱלֹהִים הוּא “this is the finger of God,” i.e. this is done by the power of God himself; pl. fingers for the hand, Psa 8:4 144:1 . As a measure across the fingers, Jer 52:21.

(2) followed by רַגְלַיִם, a toe, 2Sa 21:20. (Ch. id., Arab. أَصْبَعُ, Syr. ܨܒܰܥ, especially the fore-finger, Barhebr. p. 215, line 11.)
